title: rubus malacoides
intro: Rubus malacoides, also known as the creeping raspberry or creeping thimbleberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family, native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat a variety of health issues. The health benefits of Rubus malacoides include its ability to reduce swelling and ease digestive issues, as well as its potential to act as an antimicrobial agent. Herbal preparations of Rubus malacoides may include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support overall health and well-being.
